title: Add local CLI version provenance
description:
Add local build provenance to the CLI so humans and agents can identify exactly which `jit` binary they are running without relying on shell inspection.

## Background

The current CLI rejects both `jit --version` and `jit version`, which makes installed binary provenance opaque. The first slice intentionally stays local and deterministic: it does not check GitHub releases, PATH conflicts, or whether the binary matches the current repository checkout.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `jit --version` prints the package version and local build provenance in a concise human-readable form.
- [ ] `jit version` prints crate version, short commit hash, full commit hash, dirty flag, build profile, build timestamp, and target metadata when available.
- [ ] `jit version --json` returns the same provenance in a stable machine-readable structure.
- [ ] Builds outside a Git checkout still succeed and report explicit unknown/unavailable provenance values rather than failing.
- [ ] Tests cover human output, JSON output, and missing Git metadata behavior.
